org.name: Lances-Team
org.rationale: Knox is the secure knowledge repository for Lances-Team.

projects.name: Knox — System Development
projects.description: Build history of the Knox knowledge repository system. See GitHub for full manual content.

manuals.title: How to Start a Session
manuals.description: Quick reference guide for all team members on starting a Knox session in Claude or ChatGPT.
manuals.author: Lance

sections.title: Step 1 — Sign In
sections.content:
Sign in at lance-knox.netlify.app
Use Face ID, Touch ID, or your access code.
First time: Redeem Invitation → paste token → set up access.

sections.title: Step 2 — Download Session File
sections.content:
Go to github.com/lance505504-tech/Knox
Click CLAUDE_SESSION.md (for Claude) or CHATGPT_SESSION.md (for ChatGPT)
Click the download button (down-arrow, top right of file view)
Save to your computer

sections.title: Step 3a — Claude Session
sections.content:
1. Go to claude.ai — open a new conversation
2. Attach CLAUDE_SESSION.md (paperclip icon)
3. Type: load session
4. Claude fetches projects, manuals and dev log — confirms what is loaded

sections.title: Step 3b — ChatGPT Session
sections.content:
ChatGPT Plus or Pro required. Browsing (globe icon) must be enabled.
1. Go to chatgpt.com — new conversation
2. Attach CHATGPT_SESSION.md
3. Type: load session
4. ChatGPT browses GitHub URLs and loads data
If it says cannot browse: start a new conversation and try again.

sections.title: Saving Work Back to GitHub
sections.content:
At session end, AI outputs updated files. To save:
1. Copy the file content from the AI
2. Go to the file on GitHub
3. Click pencil icon to edit
4. Ctrl+A, paste
5. Click Commit changes — GitHub saves and triggers automatic backup

OR use knox-update.py (see Development Tools manual) to push via script.

manuals.title: Knox Data Rules
manuals.description: What can and cannot go in Knox. The simple rule: personal data goes in a private repo, everything else can go in Knox.
manuals.author: Lance

sections.title: The Simple Rule
sections.content:
If it contains personal data — it goes in a private repository, not Knox.
Everything else can go in Knox without restriction.

sections.title: What is Personal Data
sections.content:
Personal data = any information that identifies or could identify a real living person:
- Full name combined with any other detail (address, DOB, phone)
- NHS number, care ID, employee number
- Medical, clinical or care information linked to a named individual
- Financial information linked to a named individual
- Staff records, HR files, payroll, DBS records
- Next of kin or family contacts

If unsure — treat it as personal data.

sections.title: What Can Go in Knox
sections.content:
Real project data that does not identify a living individual:
- Procedures, policies, process documents
- Operational data, statistics, aggregated figures
- Templates, forms, structural documents
- Code, scripts, technical development work
- Training materials and guides
- Reference information, protocols, standards
- Project plans, timelines, meeting notes
- Financial data not linked to an individual

Real data, real projects, real figures — fine in Knox as long as no individual is identifiable.

sections.title: Credentials — Always Private
sections.content:
Never in Knox under any circumstances:
- Passwords or PINs
- API tokens or access keys
- Any credential that grants access to a system

sections.title: The One-Question Test
sections.content:
Before committing anything to Knox:

'Does this file contain information that identifies a real living person?'

  No  → commit to Knox
  Yes → store in private repository, reference from Knox

manuals.title: Development Tools
manuals.description: Scripts for pushing Knox files to GitHub. knox-update.py is the primary tool.
manuals.author: Lance

sections.title: knox-update.py
sections.content:
Primary tool for pushing Knox files to GitHub via API.
Pushes: dev-log.md → logs/dev-log.md and notes.md → active/notes.md

Usage:
  1. Save knox-update.py, dev-log.md and notes.md in the same folder
  2. Run: python knox-update.py
  3. Enter GitHub Personal Access Token when prompted
  4. Files pushed — Knox backup workflow fires automatically (30 versions kept)

Token: github.com/settings/tokens — needs repo + workflow scopes
Never paste tokens in chat.

sections.title: Pushing repository.json
sections.content:
repository.json must be pushed separately (knox-update.py only handles dev-log and notes).

Options:
  A. GitHub web editor: navigate to repository.json in repo → pencil icon → paste → commit
  B. Extend knox-update.py to include repository.json in the file list
  C. knox_push.py (extended version) — handles all three files including repository.json

repository.json push triggers the backup workflow the same as other files.

projects.name: Cape 31 Class Management System
projects.description: Single-file HTML/JS class management system for Cape 31 International One Design.

manuals.title: Infrastructure & URLs
manuals.description: Environments, repos, URLs, deploy commands, Azure workflow, common problems.
manuals.author: Lance

sections.title: Environments
sections.content:
PRODUCTION
  URL:   https://proud-pebble-0896e5503.2.azurestaticapps.net
  Repo:  cape31one-sudo/Cape31-azure
  Local: C:\Cape31\

DEVELOPMENT
  URL:   https://polite-bay-0d57fd203.6.azurestaticapps.net
  Repo:  cape31one-sudo/Cape31-Development
  Local: C:\Cape31\Development\

Accounts: Azure=Cape31one@gmail.com  GitHub=cape31one-sudo

sections.title: Deploy Commands
sections.content:
DEV:
  cd C:\Cape31\Development
  copy cape31-class-management.html index.html
  git add .
  git commit -m "message"
  git push -f https://cape31one-sudo:TOKEN@github.com/cape31one-sudo/Cape31-Development.git master:main

PROD (only after dev confirmed):
  cd C:\Cape31
  copy cape31-class-management.html index.html
  git add .
  git commit -m "message"
  git push -f https://cape31one-sudo:TOKEN@github.com/cape31one-sudo/Cape31-azure.git master:main

TOKENS: github.com/settings/tokens — needs repo + workflow scopes
Never paste tokens in chat — revoke immediately if exposed
Audit before every deploy: python3 cape31_audit.py cape31-class-management.html

sections.title: Azure Workflow — Critical
sections.content:
Working workflow uses OIDC authentication. Key settings:
  output_location: ""  (NOT / and NOT .)
  skip_app_build: true
  Uses github_id_token from OIDC step

Workflow files:
  Prod: C:\Cape31\.github\workflows\azure-static-web-apps-proud-pebble-0896e5503.yml
  Dev:  C:\Cape31\Development\.github\workflows\azure-static-web-apps-polite-bay-0d57fd203.yml

Secret names:
  Prod: AZURE_STATIC_WEB_APPS_API_TOKEN_PROUD_PEBBLE_0896E5503
  Dev:  AZURE_STATIC_WEB_APPS_API_TOKEN_POLITE_BAY_0D57FD203

Full working YAML in CAPE31_DEV_NOTES.md — use that if workflow ever needs rebuilding.

sections.title: Technical Facts
sections.content:
Single HTML file app — all HTML, CSS, JS in one file. No build step.
Storage keys: c31_cms_v1 (main), cape31_users_v1, cape31_buttons_v3, cape31_demo_v1
HAS_CLOUD = false — localStorage only

CRITICAL: Always download working file from Claude. NEVER copy from live site.
Cloudflare obfuscates email addresses in served HTML — breaks login.

sections.title: Common Problems
sections.content:
Login not working: Cloudflare email obfuscation — use file from Claude not live site
doLogin undefined: JS syntax error — run audit, check braces/backticks/escape sequences
renderStats crash: stats-bar null on mobile — add null guard
Azure token invalid: Portal > Static Web App > Manage deployment token > copy fresh > update GitHub secret

manuals.title: Architecture & Build Phases
manuals.description: Three-tier structure, role model, entry gates, build sequence.
manuals.author: Lance

sections.title: Three-Tier Structure
sections.content:
INTERNATIONAL (scope=international): Boat register, sail buttons, build certs, IRC certs, measurement, int'l calendar, user access

REGIONAL (scope=uk|eu|sa):
  UK: ~35 boats, full ExCom, buttons required, class fee active
  EU/Med: ~15 boats, building
  South Africa: ~10 boats, emerging
  Manages: regattas, entries, results, fees, yacht clubs

YACHT CLUBS (scope=[club_id]):
  Event-scoped per regatta. Entry list, provisional results, document upload.
  Results provisional until ExCom publishes.

sections.title: Role & Permission Model
sections.content:
ROLES (universal): admin | excom | owner | member | club
SCOPE: international | uk | eu | sa | [club_id]
PERMISSIONS (additive): finance, buttons_admin, cert_admin, user_admin, results_publish, entry_confirm

ENTRY GATE RATCHET:
  Regional admin turns ON (fee/insurance/address) with confirmation popup
  Only Int Admin turns OFF — audit logged
  Default: all OFF. UK=all ON.

sections.title: Build Sequence
sections.content:
Phase 1 COMPLETE: Boat register, fees, regattas, entries, results, sail buttons, payments
Phase 2b COMPLETE: Scope/permissions, entry gate toggles, regatta flags
Phase 2c IN PROGRESS: Club accounts (club record, club user role, club login view, provisional results, ExCom publishes)
Phase 2d NEXT: IRC cert, handicap results, Vakaros CSV import
Phase 3: Noticeboard, WhatsApp/Telegram push, email notifications, public results
Phase 4: Region management, int'l dashboard, builder registration

manuals.title: Role Access Matrix
manuals.description: Official access matrix — single source of truth. Consolidated from 4 Excel files (all consistent).
manuals.author: Lance

sections.title: Access Matrix
sections.content:
ROLES: Admin | ExCom | Owner/Manager | Member
Source: 4 Excel files uploaded April 2026 — all consistent.

USER MANAGEMENT
  Add/remove users, set roles, link boats:   Admin only
  Approve access requests:                   Admin only

SAIL BUTTON REGISTER
  View entire fleet & all buttons:           All roles
  Issue standard 7 buttons (on fee payment): Admin ONLY — not ExCom
  Issue bonus buttons (+2 multi-territory):  Admin ONLY — not ExCom
  Amend/correct a button (written reason):   Admin + ExCom — audit logged
  Enter IHC cert number (once, then locked): Admin + ExCom + Owner/Manager
  View audit log (tamper-proof):             Admin + ExCom

REGATTAS & ENTRIES
  Create & manage regattas:                  Admin + ExCom
  Enter own boat in regatta:                 Admin + ExCom + Owner/Manager
  Add/edit crew for own entry:               Admin + ExCom + Owner/Manager
  View all regatta entries & results:        All roles

CLASS FEES & FLEET
  Confirm fee payment (triggers buttons):    Admin + ExCom
  View class fees status:                    All roles
  View race results & standings:             All roles
  Add/edit fleet register:                   Admin + ExCom

KEY RULES
  Owner/Manager: Same role — boat manager linked to specific hull they don't own
  OSR HW Jibs: No button required, unlimited per class rules G.2
  Buttons: 7/year (max 1 main, 3 headsail, 3 spinnaker)
  Bonus: +2 any type if 6+ events across 2+ RCA territories. Admin applies on request.
  IHC cert: Manufacturer cert number from build cert. Entered once, locked on save.
    Admin/ExCom can amend — mandatory written reason — permanently audit logged.

sections.title: Build Implementation
sections.content:
canManageButtons() = isIntl() && isAdmin() ONLY — not ExCom
ExCom can AMEND buttons (with reason) but cannot ISSUE them

IHC cert number:
  Label: 'IHC certificate number (from build cert)'
  Owner enters once — certNumberLocked=true on save
  Locked field shows padlock, read-only
  Admin/ExCom amend: prompt for written reason → audit log entry

Boat Manager: positions=['boat_manager'], managedBoatId field, same permissions as Owner

Audit log fields: action, userId, userName, boatId, beforeValue, afterValue, reason, timestamp

manuals.title: Stripe & Azure Functions
manuals.description: Stripe payment integration. Azure Functions API from cape31-azure.zip.
manuals.author: Lance

sections.title: Azure Functions
sections.content:
cape31-azure.zip contains API folder with Azure Functions:
  create-checkout/index.js — creates Stripe Checkout sessions
  get-payment-status/index.js — queries Stripe for payment status
  stripe-webhook/index.js — receives Stripe payment confirmations

ENV VARS in Azure Portal > Static Web App > Configuration:
  STRIPE_SECRET_KEY = sk_live_...
  STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET = whsec_...
  SITE_URL = https://YOUR-SITE.azurestaticapps.net

Webhook endpoint: https://YOUR-SITE.azurestaticapps.net/api/stripe-webhook
Events: checkout.session.completed, checkout.session.expired, payment_intent.payment_failed

Test card: 4242 4242 4242 4242, any future expiry, any CVC
Use sk_test_ while testing, sk_live_ for production
